title: Fix Internal Server Error from AWS API Gateway Endpoint
description:
## Context

Intermittent internal server errors (500) were occurring from the AWS API Gateway endpoint (`https://hkv22n88tc.execute-api.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/dev`) when processing Salesforce dashboard component and report metadata payloads. The errors returned generic server error messages instead of actionable error information.

## Issue

Large JSON payloads containing Salesforce dashboard configurations (components, report metadata, extended metadata) were intermittently triggering 500 errors from the API Gateway/Lambda backend. The error responses lacked appropriate messaging to help diagnose the root cause.

## Resolution

This issue was ultimately resolved through related work on the metrics analyst feature set. The underlying API infrastructure was improved as part of broader error handling and reliability efforts.
acceptance_criteria: API Gateway endpoint returns descriptive error messages instead of generic 500 errors
Large Salesforce dashboard payloads are processed without intermittent failures
Error responses include sufficient detail for debugging
middleware_changes: Error handling improved in the Lambda function processing Salesforce dashboard payloads to return meaningful error messages.
api_changes: AWS API Gateway endpoint error responses updated to return descriptive JSON errors instead of generic 500 pages.
implementation_notes: The issue involved the AWS API Gateway/Lambda endpoint used for processing Salesforce dashboard and report metadata. The payload example in the ticket shows a complex nested structure of dashboard components, report metadata, and extended metadata. The issue was ultimately resolved as part of other metrics analyst work rather than as a standalone fix.
comment_summary: Ryan noted the issue was no longer relevant and marked it as resolved, as it had been addressed through related metrics analyst tickets.
